title: MLS lineup/XI-strength adjustment — walk-forward (NEGATIVE result)
question: Does adjusting a team rating by its starting XI player strength (summed prior xG/90, vs the team usual XI) beat team-xG (M3) out of sample?
method: leak-free walk-forward n=162; reconstructed XI = top-11 by minutes; player strength = recency-weighted xG/90 from prior matches; lambda scaled by (XI/usual)^gamma; gamma=0 == M3.
verdict: NEGATIVE / marginal. Best +0.0008 (vs xG +0.0071, win% +0.0086) and NON-monotonic (degrades to -0.0094 at full weight). Team xG already encodes who played. Do NOT ship an XI-strength probability adjustment. Player data is best used as DISPLAY context (released XI, key absences) and revisited for (a) targeted key-absence rules, (b) GK/defence effects (untested), (c) larger samples.
caveats: Tests attacking XI strength only (xG-based); GK/defence untested; averaged over all matches so rare high-impact absences are diluted; realized top-11-by-minutes stands in for announced XI.
